principle: Agent guides. Learner decides. Learner touches real tools. Agent verifies.

preflight_day.title: Orientation + Commitment + GitHub Workspace
completion_gate: git worktree exists
AGENTS.md exists at repo root
onemillion-builder/course-manifest.json exists
origin points to learner fork
upstream points to siddsdixit/teach-one-million
learner confirms upstream repo is starred
learner answers Day 0 reflection questions
learner made public or private commitment
git worktree exists
AGENTS.md exists at repo root
onemillion-builder/course-manifest.json exists
origin points to learner fork
upstream points to siddsdixit/teach-one-million
learner confirms upstream repo is starred

qa: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set locally and in Vercel
auth redirect URLs updated if used
Builder Claim form or fallback submitted

days.title: Idea Agent + PRD Draft
learning_focus: understand what makes a good idea
describe user pain and unmet need
completion_gate: .onemillion/project.json
.onemillion/prd.md includes idea brief and first PRD
learner reviewed and saved PRD

days.title: Validate The PRD
learning_focus: review and edit the Day 1 PRD
separate full product vision from MVP
human_decisions: whether to keep, refine, or pivot
completion_gate: .onemillion/prd.md includes Day 2 Learner Review
.onemillion/prd.md includes validation evidence
.onemillion/prd.md includes competitors/workarounds
.onemillion/prd.md includes ICP and TAM/SAM/SOM sanity
.onemillion/prd.md includes full product versus MVP
.onemillion/prd.md includes Keep/Refine/Pivot verdict

days.title: Lock The Spec
learning_focus: turn validated PRD into a buildable spec
break MVP into CRUD building blocks
completion_gate: .onemillion/refined-prd.md exists
.onemillion/refined-prd.md includes Day 3 Locked Spec
functional requirements listed
core MVP entities and CRUD actions listed
features tagged MVP or POST-MVP
complete core flow listed
data schema and business rules listed
exactly 3 core user stories
2+ use cases
testable acceptance criteria
2+ KPIs/success signals
locked out-of-scope list
concrete definition of done

days.title: Design The Product
learning_focus: turn refined PRD into screens and flows
explain what good product design means
choose design direction based on user audience and product type
decide mobile-first, desktop-first, or balanced responsive behavior
define MUI / Material Design 3 design language
design loading, empty, error, partial, full, and success states
completion_gate: .onemillion/design-spec.md exists
.onemillion/design-system.md exists
.onemillion/screens/ contains main screen specs
.onemillion/seed-data.json exists
main screens and states are described
loading, empty, error, partial, full, and success states are covered
mobile and desktop behavior are described
MUI / Material Design 3 component patterns are named
learner approved the design direction

days.title: Plan Architecture
learning_focus: decide product type: web app, mobile-first responsive app, agent, or hybrid
choose frontend, backend, database, and AI boundaries
choose backend path: Supabase/Next.js by default, FastAPI plus Railway/Fly.io/Render only when justified
design secure architecture with auth, session handling, authorization, secrets, RLS, AI permissions, and rate/cost limits
decide single-user, multi-tenant, or public/community data model
decide whether RBAC is needed and define roles if it is
factor scalability, compliance, privacy, reliability, observability, cost, and integrations into architecture
create small self-contained sprint briefs as build contracts
human_decisions: backend path: Supabase-only or FastAPI with selected backend host
completion_gate: .onemillion/architecture.md exists
.onemillion/sprints/ exists
product type decision is recorded
backend path decision is recorded
tenancy model is recorded
RBAC decision is recorded
security model is recorded
data ownership boundaries are recorded
sprint briefs are small self-contained build contracts
validate-plan passes or findings are resolved

days.title: App Shell + First Deploy
learning_focus: create the app from the plan
verify live source matches local code
completion_gate: GitHub product repo exists
local app runs
Vercel deployment URL returns 200
live homepage matches local source markers

days.title: Auth + Database
learning_focus: explain why authentication matters for trust, privacy, personalization, billing, audit trails, and safe AI actions
understand identity, session, authorization, and RLS
plan single-tenant vs multi-tenant user hierarchy from the beginning
create first tables with ownership fields
create profile, organization, membership, and role tables when needed
human_decisions: profile, organization, membership, and role hierarchy
completion_gate: Supabase project exists
auth method is chosen and implemented
single-tenant or multi-tenant model is chosen intentionally
RBAC roles are defined when teams, organizations, or admin/member differences exist
signup works locally and live when signup is part of the product
login/logout works
protected routes reject unauthenticated users
RLS enabled on private tables
second-user isolation passes when private data exists
env vars are not leaked

days.title: Core Build
learning_focus: use the sprint brief as the build contract
build CRUD or archive from sprint brief
handle loading, empty, error, success, and permission states
ship and verify the main non-AI workflow
human_decisions: which sprint brief is the Day 8 target
CRUD or archive actions in scope
states that must be visible today
completion_gate: exactly one useful sprint is selected and completed
core create/read/update/delete or archive works locally
data persists in Supabase
loading, empty, error, and success states are handled
protected routes still reject unauthenticated users
second user cannot see first user data when private data exists
deployed app works after the feature is pushed
live deployed workflow matches the local source/build
one meaningful sprint commit exists

days.title: Implementation Review
learning_focus: inspect UI manually against product intent
review the flow from the target user's shoes
assess whether the sprint alleviates the original pain point or unmet need
classify blockers, bugs, gaps, edge cases, and observations
human_decisions: whether the UI looks like the learner had in mind
whether the flow is simple and natural
whether the target user would understand what to do
whether the Day 8 sprint alleviates the pain point
completion_gate: .onemillion/review-findings.md exists
manual UI/product inspection is included
target-user pain-point fit is assessed
blockers are fixed or explicitly deferred with reason
deferrals have a next sprint or reason
code still builds
Day 8 core workflow still works locally or live

days.title: QA + Tests
learning_focus: understand manual QA versus automated QA
generate a test plan and test cases
write behavior checks from acceptance criteria
understand backend testing when a backend service exists
understand Playwright and Selenium-style E2E testing
test happy paths and edge cases
human_decisions: which tests should be automated now
which checks can remain manual for MVP
completion_gate: .onemillion/test-plan.md exists
acceptance criteria are mapped to manual or automated test cases
.onemillion/test-results.md exists
manual QA checklist is completed
automated tests run where the repo supports them
auth/RLS/tenant/RBAC permission checks are covered
live app passes critical path QA
failures are fixed or explicitly deferred with reason

days.title: AI Feature Spec
learning_focus: understand adding AI means calling a large language model inside the app to do a specific job
understand simple AI feature versus agentic behavior
understand API key basics for Anthropic, OpenAI, and Google
understand tool calling at a high level
understand AI frameworks at a high level and why to skip them for now
define failure modes, privacy, latency, and cost budget
human_decisions: simple AI feature versus agentic workflow
what data the AI can see
completion_gate: refined PRD includes the selected AI feature
provider/model choice is recorded
API key and secret-storage plan is recorded
input/output contract is recorded
tool-calling decision is recorded
AI behavior is measurable
failure modes, privacy boundary, latency, and cost budget are recorded

days.title: First AI Build
learning_focus: call the selected LLM from server-side code
handle selected provider API keys safely
store keys in .env.local and Vercel env vars
build a Next.js route handler or server action for the AI call
build prompt inputs from product data
show AI output in the app
handle missing key, invalid key, rate limit, timeout, and bad response errors
human_decisions: selected provider and env var name
what product data is safe to send
whether output is displayed, saved, reviewed, edited, or acted on
completion_gate: AI route or server action exists
AI output appears in app
selected provider key is stored in .env.local
selected provider key is stored in Vercel env vars
.env.example has placeholders only
API key is not exposed to client code
local app AI path works
live app AI path works
common error states are handled

days.title: Product Polish + UX Finish
learning_focus: inspect the app from the target user's point of view
improve copy, hierarchy, labels, and navigation
add empty, loading, error, success, and partial states
make the AI feature understandable, retryable, and reviewable
fix mobile and desktop polish issues
update existing refined PRD/spec only if intended behavior changes
human_decisions: UX problems to fix now versus defer
completion_gate: main MVP flow is easy to understand without explanation
empty/loading/error/success states are present where needed
AI feature has clear waiting, output, retry, and review behavior
mobile and desktop layouts do not overlap or break
target-user pain point still feels central

days.title: Security + Trust Review
learning_focus: review owner-based access, tenancy, and RBAC
review AI data privacy and minimal context
decide whether RAG/tool use is skipped or justified
check cost, rate-limit, and abuse controls
human_decisions: single-tenant, multi-tenant, or public/community data stance
completion_gate: auth routes and protected pages behave correctly
owner, tenant, or RBAC checks exist where needed
Supabase RLS policies protect user/tenant data
no server-only secret is exposed to client code or git
AI route receives only allowed, minimal context
RAG/tool use is skipped or justified by the product
cost/rate/abuse controls are present or consciously deferred with reason
critical trust blockers are fixed before Day 15

days.title: QA + Production Readiness
learning_focus: turn acceptance criteria into final QA
run manual QA and automated tests where useful
test auth, data, AI, and live deployment critical paths
classify blockers, fix-now items, accepted MVP risks, and deferrals
human_decisions: critical paths that must pass before shipping
which tests should be automated now
which tests can remain manual for MVP
AI pass/fail examples and quality threshold
completion_gate: local build/test commands pass or failures are understood and fixed/deferred
manual QA covers the main user journey, auth, data, AI feature, and error states
automated tests cover the highest-risk behavior the repo can support
live deployment is checked for the critical path
AI pass/fail examples produce acceptable behavior
no Day 14 critical security/trust blocker remains
production-blocking bugs are fixed before Day 16
accepted risks are explicit in the existing state/verification trail

days.title: Ship Production
learning_focus: Supabase Auth Site URL and redirect URL checks
optional FastAPI backend deployment only if architecture selected it
human_decisions: optional FastAPI backend host if architecture selected FastAPI
completion_gate: production URL returns 200 and does not show an application error
live app matches the current local/product source with a unique product marker
Vercel production environment variables are set by name, with no secret values committed
Supabase Site URL and redirect URLs include the production URL where auth is used
RLS/auth/tenant assumptions from Day 14 still hold in production
optional FastAPI backend is deployed and health-checked, or explicitly skipped because architecture did not select it
live smoke test covers the main flow, auth, data, and AI feature where applicable
monitoring/analytics/uptime is configured or intentionally deferred with reason
custom domain works or is explicitly skipped
rollback path is known and accessible
.onemillion/state.json includes the production live_url

days.title: Brand + Marketing + Pricing + First Users
learning_focus: launch content for LinkedIn, X, and communities
human_decisions: which feedback changes product, copy, or Builder Claim
completion_gate: positioning statement is specific and benefit-first
landing/product page has headline, pain, solution, proof/demo, CTA, and basic SEO metadata
pricing stance is explicit, even if it is free beta
outreach list includes 5-10 real people or relevant communities
at least 3 direct outreach messages or posts are sent, or queued with a concrete send time
every outreach message includes the live URL and a clear ask
feedback signal or pending-response status is recorded honestly

days.title: Demo Day + Builder Claim
completion_gate: public Loom URL
live app URL
Builder Claim issue submitted
.onemillion/state.json contains final verification summary
